title: Ethereum Layer 2 TVL Hits a Record $47B
description: Layer 2 solutions on Ethereum have reached a new record with total value locked (TVL) above $47B, up 23% over the past month. Arbitrum continues to dominate the space with more than $18B in TVL, while Base and Optimism are showing significant growth in DeFi adoption.
lead: Value locked in Layer 2 solutions grew 23% over the month
author: Editorial

children.value: solutions on Ethereum have reached a new record with total value locked (
, up 23% over the past month. Arbitrum continues to dominate the space with more than $18B in TVL, while Base and Optimism are showing significant growth in DeFi adoption.
The jump in Layer 2 TVL comes amid a mass migration of
to more cost-efficient scaling solutions. The Arbitrum ecosystem has benefited especially from deployments of major protocols:
, the Layer 2 solution from Coinbase, has become a strong competitor with $2.8B in TVL. The network focuses on consumer applications and has attracted significant liquidity:
Seamless integration with fiat on-ramps lowers the barriers for traditional users
keeps third place with $7.2B in TVL, thanks to established protocols such as Synthetix and Velodrome. The recent Bedrock upgrade improved transaction finality and reduced costs.
The growth reflects broader institutional adoption of Layer 2 solutions.
protocols are increasingly choosing Layer 2 networks for tokenization projects due to lower operating costs.
MakerDAO's recent proposal to deploy additional DAI liquidity on Arbitrum and Base signals institutional confidence in the long-term viability of these scaling solutions.
The surge in Layer 2 TVL points to a maturing
where users prioritize cost efficiency over network effects. The trend suggests continued migration away from Ethereum mainnet, which could lead to lower gas-fee revenue for Layer 1.

The concentration of liquidity in three major Layer 2 networks creates both opportunities and risks. While competition drives innovation, it also fragments liquidity and creates potential systemic risks.
